steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the isosceles condition
steps.content: An isosceles triangle has at least two equal angles. Since angle $U$ is $31^\circ$, one sufficient condition is another angle also measuring $31^\circ$.

steps.title: Test the matching angle
steps.content: The measure of angle V is 31 degrees. says angle $V$ is $31^\circ$. Then angles $U$ and $V$ are equal, so triangle $UVW$ must be isosceles.

steps.title: Confirm the other choices
steps.content: The other listed values for angle $V$ do not create a required equal pair with angle $U$. The measure of angle V is 31 degrees. is correct.
